title: Natick mom to run the 2026 Boston Marathon for kinder communities
author: Autumn Sloboda
bullets: Danica Champion, a Natick mother and ESL teacher, will run her first marathon in the 2026 Boston Marathon.
Champion runs to support Spark Kindness, a Natick nonprofit focusing on empowering children's health and resilience.
Funds raised aid programs combating bullying, racism, and mental illness, promoting kindness and justice in communities.

title: 10 predictions for the Red Sox this season
author: Trevor Hass
bullets: Garrett Crochet is predicted to win the Cy Young Award after finishing second last year with an 18-5 record.
Trevor Story is expected to improve his fielding after committing 19 errors last season, tied for third most in the majors.
The Red Sox are predicted to win 92 games, finish second in their division, and lose in the ALDS.

title: Developer vows to present more ‘options’ for Davis Square tower project amid neighborhood opposition
author: Molly Farrar
bullets: Developer Copper Mill pledges to present new options for a 26-story tower in Davis Square amid community pushback.
The proposed tower includes 502 apartments with 126 affordable units, facing criticism for its size and design.
Copper Mill plans to open a project office on March 23 in Somerville to facilitate community engagement.

title: He’s running the Boston Marathon for his 2 nephews with down syndrome
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Steven Ponte, from Somerville, is running the 2026 Boston Marathon for his nephews with Down syndrome.
Ponte's nephew Sebastian, who passed away, and Zachary, are his motivation to support families with Down syndrome.
The Massachusetts Down Syndrome Congress provided vital support to Ponte's family during medical challenges.

title: Boston firefighter, National Guard member reportedly injured in strike that killed 6 U.S. soldiers
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Boston firefighter and National Guard Major Mark Garcia injured in Iranian drone strike in Kuwait that killed six U.S. soldiers.
Major Garcia is recovering in Germany, with plans to return home in a few months, Governor Healey reported.
Boston Firefighters IAFF Local 718 praised Garcia's dedication to both the city and the Armed Forces.

title: Lowell murder trial delayed after lead State Police investigator allegedly causes fatal crash while driving drunk
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Lowell murder trial delayed indefinitely due to intervention of State Police investigator allegedly involved in a fatal crash.
State Police Sgt. Scott Quigley, involved in a fatal crash, allegedly had a .11 blood alcohol level at the time.
The crash investigation complicates the Phan brothers' murder retrial, initially scheduled for April 27, now postponed.

title: ​Bruins’ playoff hopes might rest on Jeremy Swayman’s shoulders for this stretch run
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Jeremy Swayman's goaltending is crucial for the Bruins' playoff chances, boasting a .906 save percentage over 42 games.
The Bruins are in the second Wild Card spot with 78 points, closely followed by Columbus at 76 points.
Boston will rely on Swayman for their last 18 games, 11 of which are away, amid Korpisalo's recent struggles.

title: Watch: Body cam shows Patriots party at downtown Boston restaurant
author: Molly Farrar
bullets: Body camera footage reveals Patriots players partying with hookahs and strippers at Estella restaurant in downtown Boston.
Boston police issued violations for after-hours drinking, unauthorized adult entertainment, and indoor smoking at the January party.
Estella owner Helder George Brandao received a three-day license suspension, blaming unruly behavior on the players.

title: 4 things to know about new Patriots safety Kevin Byard
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Patriots signed Kevin Byard to a one-year, $9 million contract after an All-Pro season with the Chicago Bears.
Byard recorded 36 interceptions in his 10-year NFL career, including a league-leading seven picks for the Bears in 2025.
Byard, who never missed a game, played under Patriots coach Mike Vrabel during their time with the Tennessee Titans.

title: Man resentenced in 2011 slaying of Dorchester teen
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Nyasani Watt resentenced to 14-17 years for 2011 fatal shooting in Dorchester, eligible for immediate parole.
Watt's earlier conviction overturned by Massachusetts Supreme Judicial Court due to denial of right to counsel.
Victim Jaivon Blake, 16, killed in gang-related shooting; another teen, Kimoni Elliot, survived with serious injuries.
